data.title: Crypto startup Brix raises $5.5 million, with participation from Circle Ventures and others.
data.description: Brix, a startup dedicated to tokenizing emerging market assets, announced the completion of a $5.5 million funding round. Participants included Yapi Kredi's venture capital arm, FRWRD, Is Asset Management, and crypto investment firms Circle Ventures, ConsenSys, and Borderless Capital. Brix plans to launch on the MegaETH network, aiming to bring traditionally large financial institutions-dominated trading strategies (such as Turkish lira arbitrage) onto the blockchain. (The Block)

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Global Silver Market Faces Sixth Consecutive Year of Supply Shortage
data.description:
The World Silver Association released its annual outlook on Wednesday, maintaining its previous stance that the global silver market will experience a supply shortage for the sixth consecutive year. According to Odaily, the report forecasts a 15% increase in the supply gap by 2026, reaching 46.3 million troy ounces. Despite an 18% rise in demand for silver bars and coins, consumption in industrial, photography, jewelry, and silverware sectors is expected to decline by 2%. Supply is projected to decrease by 2%, primarily due to a slight drop in mining output and reduced hedging, although a 7% increase in recycling will partially offset this.

The report indicates that while the Iran conflict casts a shadow over short-term price prospects, the outlook for silver for the remainder of 2026 remains "constructive." The association anticipates that the Middle East conflict will be controlled, and the tightening of monetary policy to curb energy inflation will be temporary. Even if the conflict persists, concerns over weak growth and fiscal constraints may lower real bond yields, thereby boosting interest in silver, gold, and other non-yielding precious metals. The report states, "The resurgence of safe-haven demand amid cyclical market liquidation should rekindle interest in gold and silver."

data.title: AI Startup Hilbert Secures $28 Million in Series A Funding Led by Andreessen Horowitz
data.description: AI startup Hilbert has announced the completion of a $28 million Series A funding round, led by venture capital firm Andreessen Horowitz. According to Odaily, Hilbert aims to assist businesses in automating growth-driven commercial decisions, addressing the issue of substantial AI investments that do not always yield returns.

data.title: Prediction Markets Emerge as Trillion-Dollar Asset Class
data.description:
Prediction markets are rapidly evolving into a trillion-dollar asset class, fueled by increasing regulatory clarity, advancements in crypto infrastructure, and distribution through major trading platforms. According to CoinDesk, these factors are contributing to the significant growth and potential of prediction markets in the financial sector. 

The expansion of prediction markets is largely attributed to the establishment of clearer regulatory frameworks, which provide a more stable environment for investors and traders. This regulatory clarity is essential for fostering trust and encouraging participation from a broader range of market participants. Additionally, the integration of crypto rails has enhanced the efficiency and accessibility of these markets, allowing for seamless transactions and greater liquidity. 

Major trading platforms are also playing a crucial role in the distribution and adoption of prediction markets. By offering these markets to their users, platforms are expanding their product offerings and attracting new customers interested in innovative financial instruments. This increased exposure is helping to drive the growth of prediction markets, positioning them as a significant asset class in the global financial landscape.

data.title: BNB Chain Completes 35th Token Burn, Eliminating 1.56 Million BNB
data.description: BNB Chain has successfully conducted its 35th scheduled token burn, removing 1.56 million BNB from circulation, valued at approximately $1.0021 billion. According to NS3.AI, this burn is part of the network's ongoing strategy to reduce the total supply of BNB tokens, thereby potentially increasing their scarcity and value. Token burns are a common practice in the cryptocurrency industry, aimed at managing inflation and enhancing the token's appeal to investors. BNB Chain continues to follow its quarterly burn schedule, reflecting its commitment to maintaining a balanced and sustainable ecosystem.

data.title: EU Grants Flexibility to Member States for Fuel and Fertilizer Subsidies Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: The European Union has announced that it will permit member states to have increased flexibility in subsidizing fuel and fertilizer prices. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this decision aims to mitigate the economic impact resulting from the ongoing conflict involving Iran. The move is intended to help stabilize markets and support economies affected by the price shocks. This development comes as part of broader efforts by the EU to address the economic challenges posed by geopolitical tensions. The flexibility in subsidies is expected to provide relief to industries and consumers facing rising costs due to the conflict.

data.title: Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell responds to Trump: He has no intention of resigning before the investigation concludes.
data.description: US President Donald Trump stated that he will dismiss Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell if he remains in office after his term expires. Powell's term as chairman is scheduled to end on May 15. In an interview, Trump said, "If he doesn't leave, then I'll have to fire him." It is understood that Trump's nominee, Walsh, has not yet been confirmed by the Senate, meaning that Powell may continue to serve as "interim chairman" during the transition period. Powell has previously stated that he will remain in office in accordance with the law if a successor is not confirmed. In addition, the US Department of Justice is currently investigating the renovation project at the Federal Reserve headquarters. Trump has repeatedly criticized Powell for not lowering interest rates as he wished and questioned the related project costs, claiming that there may be "corruption or serious mismanagement." Powell responded that he has no intention of resigning before the investigation is completed and emphasized that he will fulfill his duties until the investigation is "completely concluded and has a transparent conclusion." (CCTV News)

data.title: World Silver Institute: Global silver market to face supply shortage for sixth consecutive year
data.description: The World Silver Institute maintained its view in its annual outlook released Wednesday that the global silver market will experience a supply shortage for the sixth consecutive year. The report indicates that the supply gap is expected to widen by 15% to 46.3 million troy ounces in 2026. While demand for silver bars and coins is expected to grow by 18%, demand from industrial, photographic, jewelry, and silverware sectors is declining, potentially reducing total consumption by 2%. Supply is projected to decrease by 2%, primarily due to a slight decline in mining activity and reduced hedging, but this is partially offset by a 7% increase in recycling. The report states that while the Iran war casts a shadow over the short-term price outlook, it maintains a “constructive view” on silver for the remainder of 2026. The institute expects the Middle East conflict to be contained and that the tightening of monetary policy to curb energy inflation will be temporary. Even if the war continues, concerns about weak growth and fiscal strain could depress real bond yields, thereby boosting non-interest-bearing precious metals such as silver and gold. The report writes, “This, coupled with a recovery in safe-haven demand as cyclical market liquidations occur, should reignite interest in gold and silver.” (Jinshi)

data.title: AI startup Hilbert raises $28 million in Series A funding, led by a16z.
data.description: Artificial intelligence startup Hilbert announced the completion of a $28 million Series A funding round, led by venture capital firm Andreessen Horowitz (a16z). Hilbert aims to help businesses automate growth-driving business decisions, thereby changing the current situation where companies invest heavily in artificial intelligence but do not necessarily see returns. (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve's Hamak: This is a "difficult time" for Fed policymaking.
data.description: Federal Reserve official Hamak stated that this is a "difficult time" for Fed policymaking, with inflation expectations appearing relatively well-controlled. He does not believe the labor market is a source of inflationary pressure, and supply shocks are tricky for monetary policy. (Jinshi)

data.title: OpenAI abandons its plan to directly lease the Stargate data center in Norway, and Microsoft takes over instead.
data.description: Following the suspension of its Stargate project in the UK, OpenAI announced it has abandoned plans to directly lease computing power from Norway's Narvik "Stargate Norway" data center. The project, originally planned for 230MW by UK AI cloud service startup Nscale, was intended to be built by OpenAI. Sources familiar with the matter said OpenAI had considered leasing approximately half of the facility's computing power but ultimately failed to reach an agreement with Nscale; the relevant computing power is now being handled by Microsoft. OpenAI stated it is negotiating with Microsoft to acquire Norwegian computing resources through its cloud services, a move that is financially more in line with its existing spending arrangements. (CNBC)

data.title: Federal Reserve's Hammack: Inflation Target Remains Unmet
data.description: Federal Reserve official Hammack stated that the central bank continues to fall short of its inflation target. According to Jin10, Hammack emphasized the ongoing challenges faced by the Federal Reserve in achieving its desired inflation levels. Despite efforts to stabilize prices, the target has not been met, indicating persistent economic hurdles. The statement underscores the complexity of managing inflation in the current economic climate.

data.title: Fed's Harker: Consumers Face Prolonged Period of High Inflation
data.description: Philadelphia Federal Reserve President Patrick Harker has indicated that consumers are likely to experience an extended period of high inflation. According to Jin10, Harker emphasized the challenges posed by persistent inflationary pressures on the economy. He noted that while efforts are being made to address these issues, the impact on consumers remains significant. Harker's comments come amid ongoing discussions about monetary policy adjustments to curb inflation. The Federal Reserve continues to monitor economic indicators closely to determine the appropriate course of action.

data.title: Fed's Harker: Job Market Not a Source of Inflation Pressure
data.description: Federal Reserve official Patrick Harker stated that he does not view the job market as a source of inflationary pressure. According to Jin10, Harker emphasized that the current employment situation is not contributing to rising inflation rates. This perspective comes amid ongoing discussions about the factors influencing inflation and the broader economic landscape. Harker's comments suggest a focus on other elements that may be driving inflation, rather than attributing it to employment dynamics.

data.title: Fed's Hammack: Inflation Expectations Appear Well-Controlled
data.description: Inflation expectations seem to be relatively well-managed, according to Jin10, citing remarks from Federal Reserve official Hammack. The statement reflects ongoing efforts by the Federal Reserve to maintain economic stability amid fluctuating market conditions. Hammack's comments suggest confidence in the central bank's strategies to keep inflation in check, which is crucial for sustaining economic growth and consumer confidence. The Federal Reserve continues to monitor economic indicators closely to ensure that inflation remains within target levels, balancing the need for growth with price stability.

data.title: X Launches Smart Cashtags for Stocks and Crypto in US and Canada, Eyes 'Everything App' Endgame
data.description: Key TakeawaysX has rolled out smart cashtag functionality on iPhone in the US and Canada, letting users view live price charts and crypto data directly in the appCanadian users can trade stocks and crypto through a partnership with online brokerage Wealthsimple; the trading feature has not yet launched in the USX head of product Nikita Bier confirmed cashtags are "just the first step" in making X the top destination for finance and cryptoPolymarket partner Tat Thang argues X is building a Web3 equivalent of China's WeChat Pay, with crypto transaction fees as the core revenue driverThe launch follows X's recent purge of crypto bots, which Thang described as a mandatory compliance sweep ahead of deploying consumer financial productsX has launched smart cashtags on iPhone for users in the US and Canada, allowing real-time stock and cryptocurrency price data to surface directly within posts on the platform -- the first concrete step in Elon Musk's push to transform X into a full-service financial super app.The new feature lets users tag a specific asset or smart contract address when posting a ticker symbol. Tapping the tag displays live price charts and related posts, bringing financial data into the social feed without requiring users to leave the app.Canada Gets Trading, US NextCanadian users have an additional capability: direct trading of stocks and crypto through a partnership between X and Wealthsimple, a Canadian online brokerage. The trading integration has not yet launched in the United States, though X head of product Nikita Bier indicated that a rollout for web, Android, and global markets is planned in the near future."Cashtags are just the first step in our commitment to be the best destination for the finance and crypto community," Bier said in a post on Tuesday, framing the launch as the beginning of a broader financial product push rather than a standalone feature. X Money Still on DeckThe cashtag launch arrives alongside X's previously announced plan to roll out X Money -- a peer-to-peer payments product featuring yield-bearing accounts and a cashback debit card -- which Musk said in March would launch in April. The cashtag feature and the Wealthsimple trading integration offer the clearest preview yet of how X's financial layer is being constructed ahead of that broader rollout.The WeChat Pay ThesisTat Thang, a partner at prediction market platform Polymarket, offered one of the more detailed frameworks for understanding X's financial ambitions, arguing the platform is attempting to build a Web3 equivalent of WeChat Pay -- the embedded payments system within China's dominant super app WeChat.Thang argued that crypto transaction fees represent the most durable revenue opportunity for Musk's everything app vision, given that subscription and advertising revenue can fluctuate and person-to-person fiat transfers carry zero margin. Crypto fees, by contrast, offer a scalable and structurally embedded revenue stream.He also pointed to X's hiring decisions as signals of intent -- specifically the addition of Bier, a Solana Foundation advisor, and Benji Taylor, former head of design at Coinbase's Base network -- alongside X's recent purge of crypto bots as evidence of deliberate groundwork being laid for a native financial product launch.

data.title: New York Fed Manufacturing Index Surpasses Expectations in April
data.description: The New York Federal Reserve's manufacturing index for April recorded a value of 11, exceeding the anticipated -0.5 and the previous value of -0.2. According to Jin10, this unexpected rise indicates a stronger-than-expected performance in the manufacturing sector. The index, which measures the level of general business conditions in New York's manufacturing industry, suggests an improvement in economic activity compared to previous months. Analysts had predicted a slight decline, but the actual figures show resilience in the sector, potentially signaling positive momentum for the broader U.S. economy.

data.title: Bitget CEO: Bitget IPO Prime's advantages lie in its clear underlying assets and compliant issuance.
data.description: In an article, Bitget CEO Gracy Chen introduced that the IPO Prime product launched by Bitget in partnership with Republic has four core advantages: Regarding the underlying assets, it holds real SpaceX equity through an SPV and tokenizes it, with Bitget acting as a participant to guarantee token redemption; in terms of compliance, Republic has a complete compliance system and rich experience in pre-IPO issuance; regarding participation methods, it supports both primary market subscription and secondary market circulation, allowing users to acquire tokens at lower prices; and in terms of liquidity, the preSPAX market capitalization is approximately $60 million, significantly higher than the approximately $5 million market capitalization of similar products, providing a better trading experience.

data.title: Canada's February Wholesale Sales Rise 2%, Below Expectations
data.description: Canada's wholesale sales increased by 2% in February, according to Jin10. This figure fell short of the anticipated 2.3% growth. The previous month's sales had declined by 1%. The data reflects a mixed performance in the wholesale sector, with the February increase not fully meeting market expectations.

data.title: The WLFI governance committee voted to burn 4.52 billion tokens and lock 62.3 billion WLFI for 2-5 years.
data.description: According to market sources, the WLFI governance committee voted to burn 4.52 billion tokens and lock 62.3 billion WLFI tokens for 2-5 years; early supporters will retain their entire allocation, while insiders will face a 10% burn.
